reviewSummary.overallAssessment: The previous playable art pack was structurally complete but visually inconsistent. The board, background, UI panels, and text-bearing surfaces were each using different material languages, causing the experience to feel assembled instead of art-directed.

primaryProblems.finding: The board suggested a crystal board, but the UI panels and background used similar decorative noise and glow treatment, so hierarchy collapsed.
Dialogue, score, and modal text sat on dark translucent surfaces with decorative streaks, reducing readability and making the interface feel busy.
Ruby, amethyst, gold, and additional glow accents appeared in too many layers at once, so the board no longer felt calm or premium.
The background contained circular glow clusters and crystal streaks that competed with the board instead of framing it.

approvedDirection.coreTheme: Obsidian / black crystal board with restrained gold veins and quiet khaki kraft-paper text cards.

boardLanguage.surfaceBehavior: dark matte-to-polished crystal with subtle reflective planes
boardLanguage.decorativeRule: thin gold veins and gold perimeter inlay only

textCardLanguage.surfaceBehavior: mostly opaque with soft paper fibers and quiet border framing
whatToAvoid: transparent patterned black plates for long text
gold body text on decorative surfaces

accentPolicy.gold: structural trim and thin vein highlights only
accentPolicy.teal: very limited utility accent only if needed for focus state

scope: procedural visual-pack generator rewritten to match obsidian + kraft direction
text-bearing CSS surfaces shifted from translucent dark glass to paper-card treatment
existing asset ids and file names preserved for runtime compatibility

board.direction: dark obsidian slab with quiet gold structural lines
restrained gold inlay and slot framing
portrait variant using the same material language

textPanels.direction: khaki kraft paper card for dialogue text
clean paper score strips with dark readable numerals
paper modal surface for rules and settings
paper winner card with state tint applied lightly by CSS

background.direction: quiet dark field that supports the board instead of competing with it

uiBehaviorRules.longTextSurfaces: Use khaki paper-card treatment with dark ink text.
uiBehaviorRules.shortCounts: Use small paper pills for pit/store counts.
uiBehaviorRules.activePlayerFeedback: Tint the paper surface subtly instead of replacing it with a glowing colored plate.
uiBehaviorRules.boardPriority: The board remains the darkest and heaviest object on screen.
uiBehaviorRules.backgroundPriority: Background should stay quieter than board and text cards.

nextRoundRecommendations.task: Refresh stone assets so ruby and amethyst pieces harmonize better with the more matte board body.
Review typography choices. Current system fonts are serviceable, but a more intentional title/display face would help complete the theme.
Create a unified coin / emblem pass so coin toss visuals match the new board language.
Reduce decorative noise in remaining tutorial/demo surfaces if they still feel more fantasy than premium.

acceptanceCriteria: Text is readable without relying on glow or extreme contrast tricks.
The board reads first, interface reads second, background reads third.
Gold appears as structure, not glitter.
Dialogue, score, modal, and result surfaces feel like one family.
The pack remains runtime-compatible with existing asset ids and manifest loading.
